transcript.text: I got sick.
Unfortunately, I did not go out or to a concert.
So I did not have a good time while losing.
Oh, no, I'm sorry.
Oh, you're fine.
Where are you based?
I live in Bosnia, Sarajevo.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I'm originally from Germany.
So my parents live in Berlin.
Yeah.
Oh, my gosh.
Yeah.
I thought that you were German because of your last name.
Yeah.
And then, but I have a lot of family.
So I have a brother, an older brother.
He lives close to Magdeburg.
Yeah.
And my younger brother and sister live in Austria.
So I am, when I go to Germany or any German speaking country, I don't speak German like fluently, but I understand and like can speak a little bit.
I'm like a B2 level, B1, B2.
But I feel like German people, I feel very at home in like Germany and Austria.
It's very.
Like, I'm very close to, like, the culture and everything.
Oh, I love that.
And, like, how did you start working for GrowthX?
Like, how did you get that job?
So I've been, honestly, I've been working for American companies and clients for a long time.
I started, I started out in, like, about four or five years ago.
I was hired by a U.S.
content marketing agency called Animals, because one of my former managers was working there, and he recommended me for a job.
And then I feel like sometimes once you are fortunate enough to get into a network of people, you just kind of, like, get these remote opportunities.
And thankfully, like, content marketing is fairly global, so you don't really have to be in, like, a specific location.
So I haven't really worked for a Bosnian company in a long time.
Okay.
Yeah, that's very cool.
Very cool.
Well, thank you for taking the time a bit earlier today.
Um, I just, oh my gosh, my voice, I sound like an alien.
I'm so sorry.
No worries.
I've been there.
I've been there.
Okay, so, um, we have, I think, like, six or seven articles in the pipeline that need to be reviewed, right, by us?
Yeah, yeah.
So, um, I, just went through an updated Airtable.
So, six articles are ready to publish, as in Amanda reviewed them, and we, um, implemented her feedback, and then six are pending review.
Um, we also updated Airtable, so it should be clear what's been reviewed and what hasn't been reviewed.
Okay, cool.
Um, I just sent those, um, to our, um, solution engineers.
Mm-hmm.
So they can just review it one more time.
Um, and, but I'm counting more than six.
Let me see.
One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten.
I have 10.
Why do I have 10?
Well, you should actually have 12.
And if you...
Oh, yeah.
Okay.
So I have 10 that need to be reviewed and two that are due to be published.
Okay, we're good.
So 10 need to be reviewed, two are about to be published.
And then I think this is like the last time where we do this like two-step review.
So once these get like signed off by our solution team, I think we can just go that Amanda reviews it and then you guys can publish it.
We'll give you access to the hotspot.
Okay.
Okay.
Perfect.
Good.
And then I owe you feedback on the images.
I will send those.
I sent them to Mark.
He's a little...
It takes him a little bit longer to respond sometimes.
So I'm going to bump it up so he can respond.
Okay.
Now I'm going to stop talking and give you the mic.
Okay.
Okay, cool.
Cool.
Right.
Okay, so that's perfect.
So two are going to be published, and then we'll update the Looker dashboard as soon as those are published.
So the five blogs that we're working on this week, they are all from the AI batch.
So we're going to prioritize this cluster until we run out of assignments, basically.
That's what we're doing.
And then we can kind of, like, cover a couple of other clusters.
But I did want to prioritize AI so we can link to your reports.
Yeah.

And then I'm also going to talk to Mark next week about the competitor cluster and tell him, like, what the benefits are and just, you know, kind of plant that seed so we can potentially start working on that.
Yeah.
Yeah, yeah, that works.
I, we can, I mean, I.
We can send over some like just articles that we've done for other clients that have performed well in ChatGPT.
But even if you go to like Perplexity or ChatGPT and ask like what are the best tools for whatever use case, you will see that the websites that get picked up are a lot of like blog posts get cited.

We also had a client, and I'm sorry if I'm like repeating this story, but we had a client who's one of their main competitors published an article about them that painted them not in a really bad light, but they pretty much said that our client wasn't an enterprise solution.
And the thing is that not only did that blog post start ranking on like the first page of Google, it was also getting picked up by LLM.
So if someone was to start asking like, oh, does this tool like help enterprise companies?
The AI would reference that article and be like, well, they're not a good, suitable solution for enterprise.
So then we had to kind of adjust our strategy and make sure that all of the blog posts that we were creating, we were saying they're an enterprise solution.
They have supported these enterprise customers.
So sometimes I think even though I understand that like some companies, like some people in leadership, they don't want to do this.
They don't want to talk about their competitors on their website, your competitors might just talk about you.
So it's better for you to control that narrative rather than let your competitors talk about you, you know, it's kind of like, it just, it's better to provide that balance.
And like you're giving these blog posts are like, so product focused.
So we can really, we can create like an entire artifact that just includes all of your like product features laid out.
And to ensure that the articles are super accurate with what your platform can currently.

And then it just allows us to give, like, LLMs a talking script for how to talk about Fieldguide and make sure that they're highlighting, like, all, you know, that they're aware of all of, like, the different features that you have.
So, yeah, I think if we can, if there's anything that I can send over, I'm happy to do that.
Yeah, so if you have, like, oh my gosh, my voice, let me just message you.
Yeah, yeah, sure.
Or actually, like, if you could just send those examples, that would be perfect.
Because, like, I also think that we need to, like, talk more about, like, product functionality.
And, yeah, so perfect.
Let's do that.
Yeah, yeah, totally.
You know, this reminded me of, not this conversation, but because you're sick.
Two years ago, I also got sick, and I completely lost my voice.
Like, I couldn't talk at all.
It was just, like, completely silent.
And, and I, and I was, I ordered food.
And.
The food delivery guy called me and I was like, and he was like, what, what's the number on your building?
And I was like, I couldn't, I couldn't get it out.
It was so bad.
I just remembered it.
I don't honestly don't remember.
I feel like I was just drinking a lot of tea, but I'm not sure if that helped.
Yeah.
That's what I'm going to do.
My mom came home today and she just looked at me.
She was like, what the hell did you do?
Do you sound like that?
Yeah.
I feel like if you, I mean, you probably like took a flight.
I feel like airplanes are just breeding grounds for viruses.
Yeah.
Okay, cool.
All right.
So we've got that out of the way.
And then because right now we are in week seven, I did mention that.
So we will provide like a summary document and notion of all the things that we did in phase one.
I know that Amanda kind of wanted us to talk about just like some
Results that we've been able to see so far.
Considering we've only published two articles so far, there's not much in terms of data to report on.
However, if you go to your Looker dashboard, so the two articles that we've published already, they are getting clicks and impressions.
So this is especially, the Google Search Console data is interesting.
So these are the two articles.
These are the clicks that they've gotten so far, the impressions.
And the average position is 6.5 for one and then 13 for the other.
6.5, it means that on average, it's on page one.
And then 13 is that on average, it's on page two, but quite high on page two.
So these are like very positive early signals because when we start publishing, we want to see that articles get indexed really quickly, automatic.

Because Google won't index something that's like super low quality or just like doesn't have a lot of content and that it gets starts ranking for the keywords that we want to target and that it's like getting obviously like impressions and clicks.
But we're, you know, once we like get additional content out, we are going to like start seeing like consistent growth week over week.
We just need to kind of build a momentum in terms of publishing.
And we need to be like every week on time with the publishing, right?
That's going to be my feedback to the team because like the review cycle took so long for these last two batches.
And that's on us.
Yeah, I think, I mean, we don't, you know, if we are late, like a couple of days, it's not that big of a deal, but it does help to be pretty consistent.
So we do deliver five per week.
So if we can publish five per week, that that's like ideal.
Yeah.
But I do have to say also, we're like in the middle, we're starting getting to into the holiday season.
So generally speaking, for all of our clients, the traffic just dips in like the last quarter towards the end of the year, because most people are out of office.
So it is something like if you notice that your website traffic is going down overall, it's probably just the holidays.
Okay.
Yeah.
sense.
Um, all right.
And then in terms of the local report.
Sorry.
So you're just going to provide me with like a summary, a notion with, okay, perfect.
Yeah.
Let me actually tag myself here.
Okay.
So then, um, I'm going to send, if you can like send that to me by tomorrow or something, then I can send that to, um, um,
Mark, I'm like totally losing my voice now.
Okay, okay, no worries.
Okay, you can, I can send that to, I'll send that over to you today.
And then, yeah, you can send that, share that with the team tomorrow.
We would, yeah, love to get, I mean, like, end of week or Monday, we would like to get feedback if you plan to continue.
Okay.
Yeah, I mean, I've been super happy so far.
So I think, like, yes, for my opinion.
So I'm going to, like, provide Mark with the opinion.
And then I will let you know what his decision is.
But I think everything has been really good.
So.
Okay, happy to hear that.
Okay, perfect.
All right.
And then I did for the additional page that Lindsay requested, I did let our analytics.
For Sunil, just to update the report with the additional information that she was asking for.
So I think he should do that today or tomorrow.
If he doesn't, I will ping him just as a reminder.
Okay, perfect.
Okay.
And I think those are all the updates that I had.
I don't want to keep you too long because you're sick.
No, honestly, that's all.
I just wanted to thank you.
Let me write it.
Okay.
Okay.
Thank you.
I appreciate it.
And then I will give, I will provide you with feedback by like Monday or Tuesday next week.
I hope that works.
Okay.
Okay.
I will let our team know.
Thank you.
Okay.
Thank you so much.
Thank you.
All right.
Get better.
And yeah, I'll see you next week.
Bye.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content pipeline, review process, and Phase 1 performance.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=128.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Pipeline:** 10 articles are in review, with 2 ready for publication. The review process will be simplified to unblock the target of 5 articles/week.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=128.0)
  - [**Phase 1 Performance:** Early results are strong, with 2 published articles ranking on page 1 (avg. pos. 6.5) and page 2 (avg. pos. 13), validating the strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [**Competitor Content:** GrowthX proposed a competitor content cluster to control the narrative in LLMs, citing a case where a competitor's article negatively influenced AI results.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
  - [**Renewal Decision:** GrowthX will deliver a Phase 1 summary today to inform Fieldguide's renewal decision, due by early next week.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)

## Topics

### Content Pipeline & Review Process

  - [**Status:** 12 articles are in the pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
      - [**Ready for Publication:** 2 articles.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
      - [**In Review:** 10 articles.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
  - [**Process Bottleneck:** The current two-step review (Amanda → Solution Engineers) is causing delays, preventing the team from hitting the 5-article/week publication target.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=631.0)
  - [**Solution:** Simplify the review process.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=199.0)
      - [**Future Workflow:** Amanda reviews → GrowthX publishes.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=199.0)
      - [**Enablement:** GrowthX will provide Fieldguide with HubSpot access.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=199.0)

### Phase 1 Performance & Renewal

  - [**Context:** This is Week 7 of Phase 1, prompting a summary document for the renewal decision.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
  - [**Early Results:** The first 2 published articles show strong initial performance.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [**Avg. Position:** 6.5 (page 1) and 13 (page 2).](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=548.0)
      - [**Significance:** This validates the strategy, as Google quickly indexed and ranked the content, demonstrating quality.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=588.0)
  - [**Renewal Timeline:**](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)
      - [**GrowthX:** Deliver Phase 1 summary document today.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
      - [**Fieldguide:** Provide renewal decision by early next week.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)

### Competitor Content Strategy

  - [**Proposal:** Create a competitor content cluster to control the narrative in LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT, Perplexity).](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
  - [**Rationale:** Competitors may publish negative content that LLMs then cite, as seen in a GrowthX client case.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=324.0)
      - [**Example:** A competitor's article claimed a client wasn't an "enterprise solution." This was cited by LLMs, forcing a reactive content strategy to counter the narrative.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=324.0)
  - [**Benefit:** Proactively publishing accurate, product-focused comparisons ensures LLMs have the correct information about Fieldguide's features and capabilities.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=401.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=771.0)
      - [Deliver Phase 1 summary document (today).](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
      - [Provide competitor content examples to Yasine.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=433.0)
      - [Update Looker dashboard after 2 articles are published.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=244.0)
      - [Confirm analytics report update with Sunil.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=771.0)
  - [**Yasine:**](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=222.0)
      - [Expedite feedback from Mark on article images.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=222.0)
      - [Present renewal recommendation to Mark.](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)
      - [Provide renewal decision to GrowthX (by early next week).](https://fathom.video/share/uu87PLWZrLRT9AcXuBzUikfgszQjAs98?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)

action_items.description: Email Mark re: competitor cluster benefits
Email team re: weekly publishing cadence
Email Yasine Notion Phase 1 summary; Yasine fwd to Mark
Email Aida feedback on Phase 1 summary
